# Letterboxd Top Four

> A free, private, in-browser tool to choose your four favorite films from your
> Letterboxd history. You upload your Letterboxd data export, pick the film you
> prefer in a series of head-to-head matchups, and get a ranked top 4 — ideal for
> filling in the "Favorite films" section of a Letterboxd profile.

URL: https://dtsykunov.github.io/letterboxd-top-four/

## What it does
- Import a Letterboxd data export (the `.zip`) or just the `watched.csv` inside it.
- Compare two films at a time and pick the one you like more.
- Get your **ranked top 4** favorites (#1–#4).

## Key facts
- **Privacy**: Everything runs locally in the browser. Your data is never uploaded to a
  server, sent to any API, or stored anywhere off your device. There are no accounts,
  no tracking, and no cost.
- **No spoilers / fair ranking**: It runs a single-elimination tournament to find #1, then
  a repechage ("losers bracket") for #2–#4. Losing one matchup does not eliminate a film,
  so the result reflects your true preferences rather than one unlucky pairing.
- **How it works under the hood**: Static HTML/CSS/JS, no frameworks. The export `.zip` is
  unpacked in the browser with the native DecompressionStream API. Film cards link out to
  Letterboxd (films can't be embedded, and posters are not fetched, to keep all data local).
- **Resuming**: Progress is saved in localStorage; you can reload and continue, undo a
  previous pick ("Go back"), and view an optional tournament graph.

## Common questions
- "How do I pick my Letterboxd favorite films / top 4?" → Use this tool: export your
  Letterboxd data, upload it, and pick winners in each matchup.
- "Is it private / does my data leave my browser?" → No data leaves your browser.
- "Is it free?" → Yes, it is a free open static page on GitHub Pages.
